GuidePage.Title: Faction Sustainability
GuidePage.Subtitle: This guide outlines the essential loops for replenishing a faction's supplies mid-round, focusing on ammunition production and medical farming.

GuideInfoBox.Title: Operational Focus
GuideInfoBox.p: Mid-round sustainability depends on two stable loops: munitions production for combat readiness and medical farming for recovery.

GuideSection.Title: Ammunition Production
GuideSection.p: When round-start supplies are exhausted, combat effectiveness depends on a steady logistical chain of lead, steel, and gunpowder.

GuideSubSection.Title: Resource Acquisition
GuideSubSection.p: There are three primary ways to gather the raw materials needed for casings and bullets:

ul.li: [bold]Salvaging[/bold] - Searching junk piles, shelves, and crates in the wastes or bunkers. This is the most efficient method in the early game.
[bold]Dismantling[/bold] - Breaking down wrecked cars or vehicles found on roads and in junkyards. This provides a significant yield of Lead and Steel scraps.
[bold]Mining[/bold] - The least viable method. Ore veins (Coal, Sulfur, Iron) have low yields and inconsistent drops, making it difficult to target specific materials.

GuideSubSection.Title: Ammo Recipes
GuideSubSection.p: Standard ammunition requires an Ammo Bench. Ensure you have the following materials inserted into the workbench:
GuideSubSection.Table: Ammo Type | Lead (Bar) | Steel (Sheet) | Gunpowder | Secondary Mat 5.56mm | 1.5 | 2.25 | 1.5 | 1 Cardboard Shotgun Shell | 0.6 | 0 | 0.6 | 1 Cardboard + 1 Paper

GuideInfoBox.Title: Workbench Note
GuideInfoBox.p: [italic]Cardboard is crafted from 2x Paper. Paper is crafted from 1x Wood Plank.[/italic]

GuideSubSection.Title: Crafting Gunpowder
GuideSubSection.p: Gunpowder is the most complex component and must be crafted via the crafting menu. There are two ways to produce it:

ul.li: [bold]Recipe:[/bold] 1x Fertilizer + 1x Coal + 1x Sulfur.
[bold]Coal:[/bold] Processed in a Forge from Charcoal (1 Plank = 1 Charcoal; 1.5 Charcoal = 1 Coal).
[bold]Fertilizer and Sulfur:[/bold] Primarily obtained through mining or faction base stockpiles.

GuideExampleBlock.Title: Method 2: High-Efficiency (Chemset and Mortar)
GuideExampleBlock.p: If you have access to a Chemical Set and beakers, you can produce gunpowder in bulk with much higher efficiency.

ul.li: [bold]1. Grinding:[/bold] Use a Mortar and Pestle (crafted with 2x Wood) to grind Sulfur, Fertilizer, and Coal (which becomes Carbon) into a liquid form.
[bold]2. Mixing:[/bold] Collect the resulting liquids into a Chemical Set.
[bold]3. Ratios:[/bold] Redistribute the chemicals into a single beaker in a strict [bold]1:1:1 ratio[/bold] (e.g. 30u Sulfur : 30u Fertilizer : 30u Carbon).
[bold]4. Final Step:[/bold] It will consume the units directly from the beaker and drop the gunpowder on the ground, allowing for rapid, large-scale production.

GuideSection.Title: Medical Supplies and Farming
GuideSection.p: Medical sustainability relies on the cultivation of Broc Flower and Xander Root.

GuideSubSection.Title: The Farming Loop
GuideSubSection.p: Farming is a precise cycle. If you miss a step, especially clipping, you may lose your ability to replant for the rest of the round.

ul.li: [bold]1. Planting:[/bold] Start with an empty planter. With a Plant Clipping in your hand, click the planter to sow the crop. Clippings function as seeds.
[bold]2. Weeding:[/bold] Use a Hoe to remove weeds. This can be done either before planting to prep the soil or after the plant has started growing.

[bold]3. Hydration and Nutrition:[/bold] Keep the water level stable. To boost nutrition, collect [bold]Dung[/bold] from Brahmin or Brahdo using a bucket or compost bag. Insert the compost bag into a [bold]Composer[/bold], then use Alt+Click or right-click to toggle the machine on. Once processed into compost, click the planter with the compost in hand.
[bold]4. Clipping:[/bold] Once the plant has grown, you must use [bold]Plant Clipper[/bold] on it before harvesting. This gives you a new clipping to replant.
[bold]5. Harvesting:[/bold] After clipping, you can harvest the plant.

GuideWarningBox.Title: Clipping Is Mandatory
GuideWarningBox.p: Each plant can only be clipped once. If you harvest before clipping, you lose the ability to replant that crop unless you find a new wild source.

GuideSubSection.Title: Seed Management
GuideSubSection.p: If your faction does not have a Seed Extractor, you must use Plant Clippers:

ul.li: Click a mature crop with the scissors to get a clipping.
[bold]Warning:[/bold] Each plant can only be clipped once. If you harvest a plant without clipping it first, you lose the ability to replant it.

GuideExampleBlock.Title: Seed Extractor Frame
GuideExampleBlock.p: To build the extractor, open your crafting menu and gather the following:

ul.li: 4x Planks
1x Cooking Pot
2x Steel Sheets
1x Duct Tape
[bold]Tool Required:[/bold] Screwdriver to finish construction.

GuideSubSection.Title: Medical Recipes
GuideSubSection.p: Once harvested, use a workbench or your crafting menu to create healing items:

ul.li: [bold]Healing Powder:[/bold] 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root.
[bold]Doctor Syringe:[/bold] 1x Plastic + 0.25 Steel, crafted at a workbench.
[bold]Stimpak:[/bold] 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root + 1x Bloodpack + 1x Doctor Syringe.

GuideInfoBox.Title: Crafting Note
GuideInfoBox.p: [italic]Empty stimpaks cannot be refilled. Super Stimpaks and Dirty Stimpaks are currently uncraftable.[/italic]
